memo:
The packet describes a closed-loop automated research pipeline with three implemented evaluation mechanisms. (1) A prior-art filter: after idea generation, ideas are checked against literature via the Semantic Scholar API and web access, and ideas judged "too similar to existing literature" are discarded (E01) — this is a hard gate on the idea stream. (2) An experimental feedback loop: results of each completed experiment are recorded as journal-style notes and the system "re-plans and implements the next experiment," repeated up to five times (E02) — an outcome-conditioned controller over experiment selection. (3) An LLM-based paper reviewer, benchmarked against ground-truth decisions for 500 ICLR 2022 papers from OpenReview (E03), whose feedback, together with completed ideas, is written to an archive that persists across iterations of the loop (E04).

Boundary and capability limits are stated explicitly by the source: the reviewer cannot query authors in a rebuttal phase (E05), and the system including the reviewer has no vision capability and must rely on textual descriptions of figures (E06). The experiment loop likewise conditions only on text (E02). Reported measurement results include a negative one: five-review ensembling with meta-aggregation "did not affect the reviewer's performance, but can reduce variance" (E07). The authors themselves withhold endorsement of outputs, advising that generated papers be treated as "hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on" rather than accepted at face value (E08).

Architecturally the salient risks are: an evaluator with known blind spots (no figures, no rebuttal) supplies the feedback that is archived and reused, so evaluator error can propagate and compound across repetitions; the reviewer's accuracy is established only against a single-venue, single-year corpus, so its calibration outside that distribution is unestablished by the packet; and the sole quality signal that reaches downstream consumers is the same reviewer the authors decline to trust. The packet does not establish any Bayesian or expected-information-gain objective, pre-registered power/sample-size criterion, formal proof checking, or independent third-party replication of generated results; those probes are answered null rather than false. Recommendations below stay within what the packet supports: measure evaluator error/bias beyond the one corpus, obtain independent evidence of figure-dependent claims given the vision gap, retain human judgment before generated content is treated as findings, and separate the reviewer's diagnostic output from any gating role given E07 and E08.

diagnostics.statement: The automated reviewer's accuracy is established only against one venue-year corpus (500 ICLR 2022 papers from OpenReview), with no other calibration set reported in the packet.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).

diagnostics.consequence: Reviewer error and bias outside the ICLR 2022 distribution are unquantified, so downstream reliance on its scores is unsupported for other domains or venues.

diagnostics.statement: The reviewer and the wider system have no vision capability and must rely on textual descriptions of figures; the experiment loop likewise conditions only on text.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality.

diagnostics.consequence: Claims whose support lives in figures or plots can be neither verified by the reviewer nor used to steer re-planning, so visual errors pass through the loop undetected.

diagnostics.statement: The reviewer cannot ask authors questions in a rebuttal phase, unlike the human process it is benchmarked against.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.

diagnostics.consequence: Ambiguities that human review resolves by dialogue are instead resolved unilaterally by the reviewer, biasing its decisions in a direction the ground-truth comparison does not control for.

diagnostics.statement: Reviewer feedback is written to a persistent archive together with completed ideas and reused as the loop repeats, while the reviewer's own limitations are acknowledged.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.

diagnostics.consequence: Evaluator error is not isolated to one iteration; it is stored and can compound across repetitions of the pipeline.

diagnostics.statement: A reported mitigation — five-review ensembling with meta-aggregation — did not improve measured reviewer performance.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.

diagnostics.consequence: Averaging over reviews cannot be relied on to correct reviewer bias; only its dispersion is reduced, so accuracy-critical gating on ensembled scores remains unjustified.

diagnostics.statement: The authors explicitly decline to endorse the scientific content of the system's output, recommending it be treated only as hints for human follow-up.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

diagnostics.consequence: Any deployment that treats pipeline output as validated findings contradicts the source's own stated confidence level.

diagnostics.statement: The prior-art filter discards ideas judged too similar to existing literature, but the packet states no similarity criterion, threshold, or measured accuracy for this discard decision.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

diagnostics.consequence: The false-discard and false-retain rates of an irreversible gate at the head of the pipeline cannot be assessed from the packet.

diagnostics.statement: Experiment re-planning is driven solely by text notes on prior results over at most five iterations, with no selection objective or stopping criterion reported.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.

diagnostics.consequence: Whether the loop converges on informative experiments or merely on ones the notes describe favorably cannot be determined from the packet.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.

success_check.observable: Reviewer decision agreement with ground-truth decisions, reported separately for the ICLR 2022 corpus and for at least one additional venue-year corpus, with error broken out by acceptance outcome.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Agreement on the held-out corpus is within 5 percentage points of the agreement measured on the 500-paper ICLR 2022 corpus, and no outcome class shows an error rate more than twice the overall rate.
success_check.evidence_needed: Per-corpus confusion matrices and agreement statistics against published ground-truth decisions for both corpora.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality.

success_check.observable: Proportion of figure-dependent claims in generated papers that are checked against the underlying result data or the figure itself by a channel outside the text-only reviewer.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: 100% of claims whose stated support is a figure receive an out-of-band check before the paper is archived.
success_check.evidence_needed: A per-paper log linking each figure-dependent claim to the independent check performed and its outcome.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.

More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

success_check.observable: Whether reviewer scores are recorded as advisory annotations in the archive versus used as an automatic accept/reject gate on what enters the archive.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Zero archive entries admitted or excluded solely on the basis of an automated reviewer score.
success_check.evidence_needed: Archive write logs showing, for each entry, the decision rule applied and whether a reviewer score was the sole determinant.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.
Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.

success_check.observable: Presence of a recorded human sign-off on each generated paper before its content is presented or reused as an established finding.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: 100% of externally released or downstream-reused outputs carry a dated human approval record.
success_check.evidence_needed: Approval records tied to output identifiers, plus a count of releases lacking such a record.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.
Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.

success_check.observable: Published specification of the similarity criterion governing idea discard and of the termination rule for the up-to-five experiment iterations.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Both rules stated in reproducible terms sufficient for an outside reader to replay a discard decision and an iteration-stop decision from logged inputs.
success_check.evidence_needed: Written specification plus logs of a sample of discard and stop decisions replayed against the stated rules.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value.
After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal.

success_check.observable: Fraction of headline experimental results in generated papers that are re-run and reproduce within stated tolerance.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: At least one independent re-run per headline result, with agreement reported and any non-reproducing result flagged in the archive entry.
success_check.evidence_needed: Re-run logs with seeds, configurations, and measured deltas against the originally reported values.
